title: Notices of Proposal and Notices of Modification - Canada.ca
content:
## Notices of proposal and notices of modification
- Notices of modification
Notices of proposal are the means by which Health Canada's Food Directorate notifies the Canadian public of its intent to amend a document that is incorporated by reference into a Marketing Authorization or the Food and Drug Regulations .
Each Notice of proposal provides a summary and rationale for the proposal and is published online for public consideration. Each notice will specify a comment period during which interested parties may provide their input on the proposal. Comments are to be sent to the contact identified in the Notice of proposal.
The notices of proposal have been divided into four categories:
- Contaminants and other adulterating substances in foods
NOP reference number
Proposal to modify the List of Permitted Food Colours (Jagua (genipin-glycine) blue)
Proposal to modify the List of Permitted Preservatives to authorize the use of rosemary extract
Proposal  to modify the List of Permitted Food Enzymes to authorize the use of  exo-inulinase
Notice of Proposal to modify the List of Permitted Supplemental Ingredients to permit 30% juice in certain caffeinated beverages
Proposal to modify the use of caffeine as a supplemental ingredient
Contaminants and other adulterating substances in foods
Proposal to transfer the maximum levels for ethyl carbamate in alcoholic beverages to the List of Contaminants and Other Adulterating Substances in Foods
Proposal to modify the Table of Permitted Nutrient Content Statements and Claims (free of sugars)
Proposal to remove the use of brominated vegetable oil from the List of Permitted Food Additives with Other Accepted Uses

## Notices of modification
Notices of modification are the means by which Health Canada's Food Directorate notifies the Canadian public that it has amended a food-related document that is incorporated by reference into a Marketing Authorization or the Food and Drug Regulations .
The notices of modification have been divided into four categories:
- Contaminants and other adulterating substances in foods
Modification to the List of Permitted Food Enzymes (transglutaminase)
Modification to the List of Permitted Food Additives with Other Purposes of Use (silicon dioxide)
Modification to the List of Permitted Food Enzymes to authorize the use of phospholipase A1 from a new source
Modification to the List of Permitted Food Enzymes to authorize the use of polygalacturonase from a new source
Modification to the List of Permitted Food Additives with Other Purposes of Use to revise the use of caffeine and caffeine citrate
Modification to the List of Permitted Supplemental Ingredients to extend the use of caffeine

Modification to the List of Permitted Emulsifying, Gelling, Stabilizing or Thickening Agents to extend the use of polysorbate 80
